hook.text: Facebook Home replaced your Android home screen with a Facebook feed. Users revolted — a 2.5 star rating in the app store.

buildup.text: Mark Zuckerberg wanted Facebook to be the layer between you and your phone. Not an app — the entire phone experience.

discovery.text: Users didn't want Facebook replacing their home screen. They wanted Facebook in its own app, on their own terms.

twist.text: Facebook Home showed that even the world's largest app couldn't force deeper phone integration. Users drew a clear boundary.

climax.text: The HTC First phone with Facebook Home pre-installed was discontinued within a month. The concept was dead on arrival.

punchline.text: Users decide how much of their phone you get. Not you.

quiz.question: Why did Facebook Home fail?
options: Users didn't want Facebook replacing their entire phone experience — they wanted it as one app
Facebook charged too much for the home screen
